nodes.label: inParm Revision 1 Parameter Set
nodes.rationale: Qualcomm ISP in-package calibration/tuning parameter files for ASUS I001D (ROG Phone 2) camera subsystem. Each file is a 199-line numerical parameter matrix with identical layout: gain/white-balance coefficients (lines 1-8), binary feature flags (10-48), noise thresholds (49-56), rotation matrix (73-108), scaling/normalization constants (113-123), and a post-processing curve (132-196).

nodes.label: Parameter Cluster A (R/G/B Gain Base)
nodes.rationale: inParm1/2/3/13/14/15 share baseline R/G/B gain profile (0.68-0.683, 0.317-0.32, 0.23-0.264) with no rotation matrix (73-84 identity pattern) and consistent white-balance feature flags. Likely represents the primary rear camera module in auto/standard mode.

nodes.label: Parameter Cluster B (Alternative Gain Curve)
nodes.rationale: inParm4/5/6 share distinct R/G/B profile (0.64/0.33/0.3) with rotation matrix and line 124 value 0/1/2 suggesting a secondary camera mode or alternate tuning path.

nodes.label: Parameter Cluster C (High-ISO / Post-Processing)
nodes.rationale: inParm7/8/9/10/11/12 share R/G/B profile (0.708/0.292/0.17) with a 65-element post-processing curve (lines 132-196) and varying normalization target values (1000/1400/2200/4000) — likely different ISO/gain stages for the same sensor mode.

nodes.name: inparm_pr2_1 parameter set
nodes.rationale: Collection of 21 device calibration parameter files for ASUS I001D; each has 199 numerical parameters controlling touch/display behavior

nodes.name: ASUS I001D
nodes.rationale: ASUS I001D (ROG Phone 2 / ZenFone 6) target device for these calibration parameters

nodes.name: Mode flag (line 9)
nodes.rationale: Line 9 values: 0.41666666 (inParm1/2/3/13-21), -1 (inParm10/11/12), 0 (inParm7/8/9) - device operation mode selector

nodes.name: Timing parameters (lines 118-123)
nodes.rationale: Line 118=420 (constant), Line 119 varies (151 or 200), Line 120 varies (1000/1400/2200/4000 - possibly scan rate or threshold), Lines 121-123=[0.125,1.5,0.33333] (constant)

nodes.name: Rotation/matrix blocks (lines 73-108)
nodes.rationale: Two 3x4 blocks (lines 73-84 and 85-96) and one 3x4 block (lines 97-108) forming rotation/transformation matrices; identity matrices in some profiles, rotation values in others

nodes.name: 64-element curve array (lines 132-195)
nodes.rationale: Present only in inParm7/8/9; appears to be a pressure-response or position-correction lookup table with 64 values ranging from -1.93 to 426.29

nodes.name: Configuration flag (line 124)
nodes.rationale: Line 124 values: 0 (inParm4/16/19), 1 (most profiles), 2 (inParm6/18/21), 3 (inParm1/2/3/13/14/15) - selects operational sub-mode

nodes.name: Trailer values (lines 197-199)
nodes.rationale: Lines 197-199 = [0.11, 2.6, 1] constant across all 21 profiles; likely format version or checksum identifiers

nodes.name: ProdX SEPolicy Boundary
nodes.evidence: Reserved for future ProdX-specific policy after domain and service contracts exist. P0-01 introduces no types, attributes, allow rules, service contexts, file contexts, or policy build inclusion.

nodes.name: ProdX Service Boundaries
nodes.evidence: Each dedicated service owns a separate directory and future process/trust boundary. P0-01 creates documentation placeholders only.

nodes.name: ProdX Audit Service
nodes.evidence: P0-07 provides the system-UID-only audit boundary for durable transaction reservation, phase/outcome recording, cancellation, health, and minimized history.

nodes.name: ProdX Audit Ledger
nodes.evidence: Single-writer, append-only, framed and fsynced ledger; every record is sequence- and hash-chain-bound. Recovery preserves corrupt or incomplete evidence and fails closed.

nodes.name: ProdXAuditService
nodes.evidence: ProdXAuditService and ProdXAuditTests are intentionally not added to a production product package set until their targeted build and device tests pass and the Authority binding is integrated.

nodes.name: ProdX Broker Service
nodes.evidence: Reserved for mediated capability request dispatch. Future code must depend on Authority and Policy decisions and may not directly own Android execution authority.

nodes.name: ProdX Extension Service
nodes.evidence: Reserved for authenticated extension discovery and lifecycle. Dynamic code loading, arbitrary plugin execution, and trust-boundary bypasses are outside the approved model.

nodes.name: ProdX Learning Service
nodes.evidence: Post-P0 placeholder only. Learning records and privacy-controlled adaptation are excluded from the P0 build aggregate.

nodes.name: ProdX Observation Service
nodes.evidence: Reserved for normalized, policy-filtered observation ingestion. Future code must enforce provenance, minimization, retention, and caller isolation.

nodes.name: ProdX Reasoning Service
nodes.evidence: Post-P0 placeholder only. Models are consumers of runtime contracts and never acquire direct Android authority. No model runtime or dependency is introduced in P0-01.

nodes.name: ProdX Test Hierarchy
nodes.evidence: P0-01 reserves separated host, device, contract, unit, integration, security, fuzz, fixture, and immutable reference boundaries. No test binary, harness, source, or build target is declared yet.

nodes.name: ProdX Contract Tests
nodes.evidence: Reserved for compatibility, canonical serialization, malformed-input, and version-negotiation tests beginning with P0-02.

nodes.name: ProdX Device Tests
nodes.evidence: Reserved for lifecycle, process, IPC, policy, audit, recovery, and runtime behavior validation on a built Android target.

nodes.name: ProdX Test Fixtures
nodes.evidence: Reserved for deterministic, versioned, non-sensitive fixtures. Contract vectors begin only in P0-02 and will use the reserved prodx-contract-test-vectors target.

nodes.name: ProdX Fuzz Tests
nodes.evidence: Reserved for contract decoders, IPC boundaries, manifests, policy inputs, and other untrusted parsers after those surfaces exist.

nodes.name: ProdX Host Tests
nodes.evidence: Reserved for source-tree, contract, build-graph, API, compatibility, and provenance checks that do not require a device.

nodes.name: ProdX Integration Tests
nodes.evidence: Reserved for cross-component lifecycle, restart, dependency, policy, and IPC validation after corresponding components exist.

nodes.name: ProdX Security Tests
nodes.evidence: Reserved for caller identity, permission, AppOps, SELinux, confused-deputy, downgrade, replay, and data-boundary validation.

nodes.name: ProdX Unit Tests
nodes.evidence: Reserved for deterministic subsystem-local tests. Unit tests must not substitute for Android boundary or end-to-end policy validation.

nodes.name: ProdX Engineering Tools
nodes.evidence: Reserved for reviewed, non-production helper tooling. P0-01 intentionally contains no script, parser, generator, binary, or executable artifact.

nodes.name: P0-07 Milestone
nodes.evidence: P0-07 provides the system-UID-only audit boundary for durable transaction reservation, phase/outcome recording, cancellation, health, and minimized history.

nodes.name: P0-01 Milestone
nodes.evidence: P0-01 introduces no types, attributes, allow rules, service contexts, file contexts, or policy build inclusion.

nodes.name: Authority Binding
nodes.evidence: ProdXAuditService and ProdXAuditTests are intentionally not added to a production product package set until the Authority binding is integrated.

nodes.name: SHA-256 Digest Bound Payloads
nodes.evidence: Caller payloads are reduced to a bounded SHA-256 digest before persistence.

nodes.name: System-UID-Only Boundary
nodes.evidence: P0-07 provides the system-UID-only audit boundary.

nodes.name: ProdXAuditTests
nodes.evidence: ProdXAuditService and ProdXAuditTests are intentionally not added to a production product package set until their targeted build and device tests pass and the Authority binding is integrated.

nodes.name: HomeLauncher Deferred SELinux Drafts
nodes.evidence: Do not apply these rules during the first ROM integration pass. The standalone app ran as platform_app. A ROM-bundled platform-signed app is also expected to run as platform_app. Previous AVCs for /proc/stat and thermal sysfs are real.

nodes.name: platform_app SELinux Context
nodes.evidence: The standalone app ran as platform_app. A ROM-bundled platform-signed app is also expected to run as platform_app.

nodes.name: AVC Denials for /proc/stat and thermal sysfs
nodes.evidence: The previous AVCs for /proc/stat and thermal sysfs are real, but new placement may expose additional denials.

hyperedges.label: inParm PR1 Camera Calibration Parameter Set
